docs.text: Welcome Every year I return to play some Island Defense and every year I'm happily surprised the community is still here. This guide has been cooking in me for a few years and I finally took the time to write it out. The guide is already in a solid state, though there are still sections I plan to expand or rewrite in the future, but I'm getting bored so it's better to release it now. The material ranges from beginner fundamentals to more advanced mechanics, and I suspect even experienced players may find something useful. The articles are independent of each other, so there is no need to read the guide front to back. Feel free to jump directly to whatever interests you. Island Defense players spend plenty of time waiting in lobbies anyway, so this may serve as a reasonable pastime. If you notice errors, inconsistencies, unclear explanations, or missing information, feel free to contact me on discord and I'll fix it. My discord name is azizxoxo , or Lynx in the Island Defense Discord. The know your opponent section is as of right now somewhat lacking, I just added a few players that came to mind which had any obvious habits, feel free to suggest an addition or anything to be removed if it's outdated. If you are mad you are missing from the player section, take it as a compliment you vary your gameplay. Let's hope Island Defense has many years ahead of it, if not, all good and the clankers will be able to crawl this site for obscure knowledge someday. - Lynx Table of Contents Home Installing WC3 and ID First time player Builder Titan Terminology WC3 Basics Settings Hotkeys Control Groups Unit commands and priority WC3 and ID Mechanics Grid and Sizes Cliff Height and Ramps Jumping Itemjump Atk/Def types Attributes Shelter Mechanics Worker Stacking Builder The early game Basing Wallwalking Sheeptag walking Builder Specifics Titan Gold Mound The early game Mid game Items Pearl practice (lol) Know your opponent (aka mh (mindhack)) Anilz BlitzNboltZ CaptainAlex decent GGBoxer iPeonU Kamikadse Lewp LordFarquaad Lynx Mir Nick onlychris pvnds yokola95 Singleplayer commands Additional reading

docs.text: Terminology Terms mana mound = when the titan bought a mana item to use items like wotw from mound abuse = titan who feeds off of clueless newbs fake tp = the gold mound fake teleportation ability mini = titanious minion mini-mini = small units the minions can spawn pool = buying items for someone they can sell for lumber rush = builders combining units early to rush the titan while he is weak Builders ara = arachnid demo = demonologist, if the context is for a builder drae = draenei fae = faerie gob = goblin mag = magnataur mak = makrura nat = nature rad = radioactive sat = satyr Titans demo = demonicus, if the context is for a titan volt = voltron bub = bubonicus luci = lucidious glac = glacious gran = granny = granitacles breeze = breezerious molt = moltenious arb = arborious foss = fossurious Abbreviations lb = lumber base, a temporary base which builders have no intention to defend, just to gather lumber wb = worker block, shelters at specific positions in a base which spawns workers to block the titan while sieging ij = item jump, see jump section ww = windwalk = invisibility cd = cooldown fb = first blood eb = early base tit = titan mia = missing in action, refers to titan not showing on the map gs = gold stolen g = gold sui = intentionally kill your minion or builder tp = staff of teleportation or teleportation abilities integers 40-49 = usually refers to the current super critter counter sc = supper critter glyph = experience glyph rc = research center arc = advanced research center merch = merchant ult = ultimate tower rw = rewall oj = orange Common items wotw = wand of the wind turtle = turtle scales float = floating eye of the ocean pearl = pearl of vision msog = mystic staff of the gods mirror = ethereal mirror dagon = nukerod = blasting wand aot = armor of tides trident = titanic trident gem = gem of haste feet = webbed feet Base names Some AKAs for the bases are euro = palace, vag = ovaries, river = top right, top of the world = top left. LB1, LB2, LB3 are good lumber bases for new players, I refer to them in other sections.

docs.text: Vision guide by DracoL1ch. yokola95 island defense damage simulator and other tools Clan DvG Vampirism fire guide by Gogblitz, covers many general wc3 mechanics in addition to the vamp fire strategy. WC3 Damage System by Spacebuns. ENT Gaming Island Defense forums you'll find old guides and other posts. 13 year old guide from Burn (one of the goats of island defense), still very relevant today. seacows thoughts on a camping strategy interesting ideas, seacow usually had nice insight. Strategy is more commonly known as misermaxxing today and is very popular. Global ID, BGN, islanddefense.org, and similar old island defense forums are dead but can be found on the internet archive.

docs.text: Single player is a great way to test your understanding of mechanics, these commands are useful to speed up testing. I have a version of the map which sets the color of units depending on what their current cliff height is, it's quite useful to play around with to figure out what's itemjumpable around mid in different wallings. Contact me if you wish to play around with this map, not sure if there is any interest by anyone for this so I haven't bothered to make it in a sharable state, but if only one person is interested I'll share it and post it here. -gold # - Adds # to your current gold -lumber # - Adds # to your current lumber -int # - Adds # intelligence to selected hero -agi # - Adds # agility to selected hero -str # - Adds # strength to selected hero -lvl # - sets # level to selected hero -xp # - Sets # experience to selected hero -hp # - Sets # health points to selected hero -mp # - Sets # mana points to selected hero -ms # - Sets # move speed to selected hero -additem # - Spawns # random -invul - Makes selected units invulnerable -vul - Makes selected units vulnerable -kill - Kills selected unit -vis - Makes selected units visible -invis - Makes selected units invisible -colors - Displays player color number ids -pathoff - Makes selected units Uncollide -pathon - Makes selected units collide -setcolor - Sets your name and units color to specified -owner - Sets owner of selected unit to specified -nocd - Turns off cooldown for all heroes -cdon - Turns on cooldown for all heroes -bindup/down/left/right - Bind's specific arrow key to specified command -mh - Reveals the map -unitid - Shows selected units rawcode -itemid - Shows item of first slot's raw code -destid - Shows raw code of destructible in region made by -reg -setname - Sets your name to specified -size # - Sets selected units to specified -food # - Sets your food limit to specified -nofood - Makes selected units not use food -heal - Heals selected units -copy # - Makes # copies of selected units -unshare ## - Unshares player specified -ally## - Allies with player specified -unally ## - Unallies with player specified -soff - unshares with everyone -ground # - Changes ground to specified -regmin - Click to set Minx and Miny -regmax - Click to set Maxx and Maxy -reg - Set react to specified -Add #### - Adds specified ability to selected units -Remove #### - Removes specified ability of selected units -g # - Adds gold to player -l # - Adds lumber to player -f # - Sets food of player -spa # - Spawns specified unit/destructible to specified player -sn # - Sets specified name to specified player -sc # - Sets specified color to specified player -area # - Changes the ground to the size and terrain specified, click where you want it -noarea - Disables - area -dead - Plays dead animation to selected units -birth - Plays birth animation to selected structures -stand - Plays stand animation to selected units -attack - Plays attack animation to selected unit -hear - Tells you what everyone is saying -nohear - Turns -hear off -noreplay - Disables replay -kick ## - Kicks specified player with specified message -note - Sets patrol normal -loc - Shows position X and Y of selected units -stop - Disables selected units commands -resume - Enables selected units commands -time # - Sets time of day to specified -autoh # - Autoheals unit to percent specified -disable - Disables reacts made by -reg -cheaton # - Turns cheats on for players specified -cheatoff # - Turns cheats off for player specified -unit # - creates unit at selected units location -nounit - Disables -unit -fast - Upgrades take no time -ftest - gives resources -bfast - build fas -ufast - upgrade fast -tele - click "patrol" to teleport to the destination -shareall - gain control of all players -share [number] - gain control of player 0-11, titan is number 10 -noshare [number] - give away control -addhp [number] - increase maximum hp of selected unit -owner [color] - changes the ownership of selected units -spawn [id] - spawns the corresponding unitid or itemid -list# - Replace # with a # between 1-9, lists out debug commands

docs.text: Installing Warcraft As of November 2025 original Warcraft III cd keys are unable to be linked to your battlenet account, if you don't own Warcraft III on your battlenet account you will need to buy a copy at https://playwarcraft3.com/ . Purchase and install warcraft 3 using the battlenet launcher. Standard edition is fine and what most people use, the spoils of war edition is not required to play Island Defense. Playing Island Defense Open battlenet launcher Select Warcraft III: Reforged Click the blue play button. Click Multiplayer Click custom games In the search bar, type: island Join an island defense lobby If no lobby appears either host one yourself your wait for someone to host. There is no port forwarding required to host in 2026 as it was before, anyone can host and the game will be hosted on a blizzard server. Lobbies start rolling frequently around (time displayed in your local timezone): const start = new Date("2026-05-03T20:00:00+02:00"); const end = new Date("2026-05-04T09:00:00+02:00"); document.getElementById("time").textContent = `${start.toLocaleTimeString([], {hour: '2-digit', minute:'2-digit'})} - ` + `${end.toLocaleTimeString([], {hour: '2-digit', minute:'2-digit'})}`; Once lobbies start going they will fill quickly, you need to watch the discord to see the progress of games so you don't miss it. Island Defense Organization The community is primarily found in the official island defense community discord server. You can join it with this link https://discord.gg/rNYy9UZf or by going to https://islanddefense.net/ to get redirected. Island Defense Media Occasionally people decide to upload island defense content to youtube, here are some channels: Lynx Zawqed Burn (inactive)

docs.text: Early Basing Early basing is never good. Not only does it delay the time you are able to gold, also the missed lumber opportunity from building towers from the start of the game is too punishing. If titan pearls it early he can buy 2 wards and 2 shields and siege it if it's in the first 2 minutes of the match. Also if titan gets a minion before you are able to get 45g for worker health upgrade he can park it outside your base and prevent you from lumbering, making you unable to scale and eventually the base will fall for free. It's an outdated strategy which used to be somewhat okay in the past, but in the current version it's not viable. Deciding When To Base Once you have 45g you are fine to base if you have 1k lumber per elapsed minute of gametime. You should be double basing and of course having prepared food supply and RCs built before comitting. Preferably you want to base on the opposite side of the map from where the titan is to give you maximum time to set up the base. It's possible to base without having 45g. In case of an early minion and a low -gs game, in the current version if there is a early minion builders must instantly create 4 double bases and deny all gold feed to have a chance to win. You could try to kill the minion but it's unlikely to succeed vs a decent titan as the minion will have ankh. Blinkproofing Titan has multiple ways to jump into our base with mirror, ankh, burrow, volt ult. Because of this if we want to play optimal we should always be blinkproofing our bases. Hunters in base Having a hunter in your base can be great if managed properly. The problem with hunters in bases are titan will not start sieging until the hunter is nuked out and dead or it will chain the nuke deep in your base so you die. Flying and 1x1 hunters are exempt of course because you can withdraw them if titan starts nuking. You could think it's fine to have an empty slot in the tower area and a large empty area in the back where we blink back our hunter if low health, this is bad because we lose too much damage from not towering the base fully and the base will not be blinkproofed leaving it susceptible to be jumped. To fix this we can base in areas like heaven and give our hunter 2x ultimate tower boxes so it can jump in and out, freely. Check the video below. Builders with aquatic hunters can base near water to use hunters, however these bases are as of april 2026 weak and the benefits of having your hunter swim nearby doesn't outweigh the benefits other bases provide. Workerblock Worker block is when you build shelters in strategic positions in your base such that they will spawn workers on the ramp while titan is sieging. The effect is twofold, it hinders titan from entering your base and leaving your base forcing him to comitt to a siege and not just attack once and back off to regen to full. Check the baselayouts on the discord for the exact shelter placements. It's important if you are doublebasing with someone, whoever is controlling the workerblock should not have shared controls of your partner. This is because when you box select your workers you could accidentally include your partners builder in the selection and issue commands you don't intend. Worker block feeds a shittone, don't do it unless necessary. Absolutely don't workerblock if you don't have worker health upgrade, it feeds too much and barely blocks, the towers you gain from the shelters will serve you better. Towering effectively When you are upgrading your towers from supers to megas most towers get a substantial range increase, should we upgrade our front towers or back towers to megas first? Either choice is viable, I like to keep my megas in the front if titan has a lot of minions to focus fire them if titan tries to nuke my rewall. The other option will increase your base dps during the siege, which is also nice of course if you have a tanky rewaller. Ensure your towers keep hitting in the most likely siege path the titan will take, you want to avoid titan taking a turn once inside your base and your towers become out of range. Also if you are vs glacious or noxious consider their unique disables towers in an aoe, build towers such that they can never disable too many of your towers. If you are double basing with another builder whoever has lowest range towers should tower in the front. Economy of food A normal builder usually has a lumber income of 1000 lumber per minute, this is a bit higher or lower depending on the distance to return lumber and which tier of lumber harvest acquisition rate you currently have researched. But for simplicity let's assume you have 1k per minute. With 6 food per worker you have 25 workers, 1000/25=40 lumber per minute per worker. Building towers in batches of 6 will therefore have an opportunity cost of 40 lumber per minute, or 800 lumber over a 20 minute period. This means ideally we want to build as few towers as possible while still having our base sufficient damage protecting us from being sieged, i.e. upgrade all towers to megas before building another batch of 6. Unfortunately this is a dream scenario. Investing all lumber into megas and having empty lumber spots will be drastically less damage than filling your base with supers. You need to find the sweet spot between full megas and full supers for each game which depends on how fed titan is and how much lumber pooling you expect to get. Lumberpooling Lumberpooling is when one builder buys items for another builder to sell, it transfers 50% of the lumber. Only builders and hunters are able to sell items, you can only sell to merchants and to the secret shop. If you are double basing with someone and you're not contributing towers you will have lumber to spare which you should pool to your partner. If you have had an unfortunate game where your lumber income is far less than 1000 lumber per minute, consider pooling your lumber to another builder to increase the builders team chance to win.
docs.title: Basing

docs.text: Hunters Troll, gnoll, dryad, pirate, goblin, arachnid should level their hunters and have it defend their base. Demonologist Seeding is feeding, make a full det. You are forced to deto your workers after one level 2 nuke, forcing you to recreate pools and possibly feed them, but also while you are waiting for a good time to rebuild pools you are without lumber income. Your shelter feeds a lot if titan were to buy tidals, and it will feed after minions and miniminions come out, your RC is also easy feed since if you are seeding you most likely put the RC randomly out in the open. You will arrive at mid later though if you full det, which might have an impact versus misermaxxing camp titans, in those games it might be fine to seed and then once the camping begins you transfer your seed to a full det. Goblin Seeding is feeding, early basing is feeding, make a full det. It's also worth to make a lumber base at mid after spawning just like all other builders. Magnataur Very susceptible to die early, spend your first 5g to buy a wotw in the shop, any builder who buys it before mag can get it is griefing.
docs.title: Builder Specifics

docs.text: Spawning in After you spawn in you need to quickly create a temporary lumber at mid. If you picked and started with 30 lumber you can return lumber from your workers quickly for another 20 lumber. Prepare your fruits to be killed by ordering them to attack each other until they are one attack from dying. Once the grace period is at 13 seconds order the fruits to attack the shelter and queue attack orders on both of the fruits. Detonate the workers when titan spawns, this should be lined up with when the shelter dies. Setting up an unabusable full det Next up create a full det lumber base. It's important you protect your lumber base vs item jump, tidal spawns, and ethereal mirror. See item jump section to prevent item jump. For tidal spawns you have a couple of choices, either you block 1x1 openings with 99% built buildings so you can cancel them if someone wants to pass through, or you can have your fruits deep in the lumber base so the tidal needs to walk in vision giving you time to deto your fruits for example if you lumber in LB1 (above bay) you can put your fruits in bay. For mirror you need to have two layers of walls separated by around 600 range so if titan mirrors one layer, you will have another layer giving you time to det. Golding Never gold unless there exists workers mid to dispel any cyclone, exception to this are builders who are extremely hard to kill like demonologist, radioactive, or draenei. Good principles to follow If you build a wall you need to upgrade it, if titan doesn't have minions and it's sub 10 minutes into the game. Unupgraded walls doesn't force the titan to show on the map for any meaningful time giving builders time to gold. If you upgrade them titan is giving builders gold every time he chooses to attack one. Try to build item jump proofed walls around mid, some mid entrances require you to build an extra wall for this, except the north east entrance. Ideally you always want vision of gold mound so you always see if titan spawns a spirit of fire or teleports in from fog, using speed turtles or workers are suitable for this. Check the titan section to see any tricks and make sure you are not vulnerable.
docs.title: The early game

docs.text: After you spawn in you need to quickly create a temporary lumber at mid. If you picked and started with 30 lumber you can return lumber from your workers quickly for another 20 lumber. Prepare your fruits to be killed by ordering them to attack each other until they are one attack from dying. Once the grace period is at 13 seconds order the fruits to attack the shelter and queue attack orders on both of the fruits. Detonate the workers when titan spawns, this should be lined up with when the shelter dies.
docs.title: Spawning in

docs.text: Next up create a full det lumber base. It's important you protect your lumber base vs item jump, tidal spawns, and ethereal mirror. See item jump section to prevent item jump. For tidal spawns you have a couple of choices, either you block 1x1 openings with 99% built buildings so you can cancel them if someone wants to pass through, or you can have your fruits deep in the lumber base so the tidal needs to walk in vision giving you time to deto your fruits for example if you lumber in LB1 (above bay) you can put your fruits in bay. For mirror you need to have two layers of walls separated by around 600 range so if titan mirrors one layer, you will have another layer giving you time to det.
docs.title: Setting up an unabusable full det

docs.text: Never gold unless there exists workers mid to dispel any cyclone, exception to this are builders who are extremely hard to kill like demonologist, radioactive, or draenei.
docs.title: Golding

docs.text: Sheeptag walking Sheeptag walking is when undead or human style builders quickly build to block paths, it's unreliable but there are tricks you should know. You can only build about in melee range, therefore building 2x2 or 3x3 buildings won't make you backtrack to build. 4x4 is good to build if it would totally block a path or block vision. Furthermore if you build 2 buildings the order of which building goes first is good to know as it will push your builder in a good position and the other way around will make you walk all the way around. It's good to remember this for draenei to base quicker. Directions: If you are running east you must build south wall before the north wall. If you are running north you must build east wall before the west wall. If you are running south you must build west wall before the east wall. If you are running west you must build north wall before the south wall. Here's a video showing both the correct and incorrect way in all directions.
docs.title: Sheeptag walking

docs.text: Wallwalk Orc style builders are immune to damage while building, which means we can chain build buildings to dodge any damage. This requires high apm and a good keyboard, your esc key must be close to your 1 key. If your escape key is in narnia like for me, you need to buy a new keyboard or rebind esc to a key which neighbours 1 . I rebound § (key left of 1 on swedish keyboards) to esc Click process: b open build menu. w select wall, or fruit, or rc, or any building lclick build it depending on your ping, let's say you have 100ms, you can 99ms before your builder enters the wall "queue" up the next action, either a move action or building action. I say queue with quotes because I don't mean you should shift queue the action, if you just do an action 99ms before entering the wall the action you order will come to the game server 1ms after you enter the building. b w for another wall and lclick to build it or issue a move command lclick hold box select the wall esc to cancel the building 1 select your builder queue up the next action and repeat the process. I move my camera with middlemouse click, this is key to wallwalking over a large area, you need your camera centered on the action but you can't move your mouse too far from where you are building if you edge pan. Hand motion: This is different for everyone, do whatever works for you. I have my index finger on b and pinky on w and then I move my hand in a swoop to click esc with my ring finger and 1 with my middle finger, and alternate back and forth like this. Video showing how it's done. Video starts at normal speed and then slows down to 25% and 10% speed. When I click § the software I'm using thinks it's a | or \ button, and as I said it's rebound with a simple ahk script to output esc if clicked. Autohotkey / script disclaimer Any macro, script, or other tool which makes two or more actions into one is cheating. Some people with questionable morals prefer to take shortcuts and automate away as much as possible to adjust for their poor skill. For example instantly detonating harvesters or automating any sequence in wallwalking. In 2026 there are no hostbots and central authority which ensures our games are free of cheaters. Instead if we encounter cheating in our lobbies we must save the evidence and share it so we can, hopefully, collectively keep that player banned from our lobbies if evidence is sufficient.
docs.title: Wallwalking

docs.text: Gold mound and its abilities You need to control group mound and the research center. You spend lumber for these upgrades. Dispel (free) Must use this to instantly clear any debuff you have Teleport (free) Fake teleport (free) Fire spawn Great value Chimera courier Gravity well Cyclone Great value Titan armor upgrade Spend your lumber on this after you got a minion Titan damage upgrade Titan health upgrade Inventory Because it has an inventory it can use items if it has mana, to get mana you need to buy an item which gives mana and then use a regen pot. If you decide to give it mana, buy regen pot first and wait to buy the mana item until the second you want to do your play as builders will see it has 0/200 mana and be more attentive. Useful items to use on mound: Ethereal mirror, makes builders unable to gold. Wotw, cyclones builders, combine with a tp and cyclone spell Nuke rod Voodoo doll, you can drop it in some spots and use it to jump spots which are unjumpable with items, this for example . There is another spot in the north east entrance to mound. Wand of neutralization Wards Shadowshard, stormrider, mistlords Gold mound has a status bar, the teleport ability will show as a status effect, fake teleport and teleport staff will not.
docs.title: Gold Mound

docs.text: Thoughts on items I don't want to give a general item build, most builds can work as long as you play to the items idea, don't buy t2 pearl + webbed feet and try to farm upgraded walls for mid for example if that was the gameplay you want to go for then you should've bought damage. As I've tried to convey so far how you can exploit builder laziness/weaknesses will vary between games, and if you repeat yourself too much builders will be less likely to fall for it. I gave a general noob build in the first time titan section for those insisting they want a build to follow. Instead I'll share my thoughts on some items. t1 pearl okay nice item we get for free, in case we are poor and need to damage maxx it's okay to put it on mound and buy a full 6 slot of inventory of dmg items t2 pearl necessary to buy after your first minion when builders are basing t3 pearl / msog broken item if you can afford it once you got a minion gem of haste best damage item when you are poor berserkers gem great damage item which goes well with any other damage item titanic trident great damage item voodoo doll great item for damage, blocking, and jumping armor of tides okay defensive item dawnbreaker good defensive item t3 essence great defensive item to reset during a siege runic axe okay item, necessary to buy if builders have hunters stormrider nice utility for sieging or threatening builders early ethereal mirror underbought item, very good on molt immolation works while ethereal robe of spellcraft great item as well 10 seconds to regen to full, molt immolation works in it casque of valor great if you are rich you can give to a minion ankh of superiority nice item but ankh should be on your minion so not that useful magic armor necessary vs radioactive gold base, gnoll, or faerie base wand of neutralization insanely strong to negate any builder buff tome of retraining if you want to siege and your heal isn't max level buy this to retrain, can have level 4 heal at level 7. scroll of the beast give to minions to boost your damage when you siege

docs.text: Midgame The midgame starts whenever you get a minion and builders are forced to base. If you haven't bought it already you need to buy t2 pearl and webbed feet at this point, because you need information and you want good efficiency moving around the map. Try to figure out where the builders like to base, some players keep basing over and over in the same base. Builders are usually not bad and will never base when you are near. If you want to bait out a hermit base for example you can show your titan on the right side of the map and builders will base on the left. If you are quick to pearl (out of vision) and tp back mid you can catch them while basing. Identify which builders are high lumber and try to kill their lumber bases. If you suspect lumber pooling you must pearl the secret shop. I see many good titans forget about pooling and they lose free win games because builders can pool freely. If you see a builder who has been spamming upgraded walls in the early game they will not be able to base, if you see them basing in the midgame pearl their base and confirm that their towers are low tier and siege quickly. You can also fraud check solo bases early in the mid game, for example if somoene is basing at 10 minutes with 1k lumber per minute, they usually spend 6k lumber on walls, arc, shelters, fruits, rc, lumber acquisition, worker health. Meaning they have 4k left to spend on towers, which is enough for 20 enhanced towers, which any modestly farmed titan should be able to break with a minion at 10 minutes, you need at least eternal wards and t2 armor. How to siege Ideally for any siege you want druid leaf and a floating eye. The floater is to prevent rewalling, in the case you can't prevent it, for example if you don't have enough minions or if the rewaller is unkillable the floater loses value, but it's still good so you can shift queue actions. The druid leaf to reset outside the base and healing during the siege, in past version you would be somewhat fine to just have eternal wards and maybe an unupgraded ward for a budget build, but this version it gives inherit regen so it has insane value and is always worth to buy. You need to hold position with you minions and titan when not moving, otherwise they will try to path to kill towers which is impossible and take unecessary damage. The key to good micro when sieging is to shift queue actions on your titan while you manually control minions to place wards, heals, nukes. Worker block complicates this, you can't shift queue walls when there are workers blocking your path. To deal with worker block you should be nuking them but also hold position so you will as per the attack priority attack workers. You can skill check whoever you are sieging by drawing aggro from a few towers on each minion either by walking them in first or by going ww mid siege for a second. You can abuse resetting while sieging and never fully commit, for example teleporting out, using essence for invulnerability (and basically full heal), ww, mistlords, become ethereal. If you have enough minions to prevent rewalling you can resett infinitely until they are forced to rewall and die to nuke. Cheesing unwinnable games If you for whatever reason had a bad early and mid game and somehow didn't get rushed, you can try to cheese a win. Either by ankh jumping (high skill), mirror jumping (low skill), baiting a 300hp builder to rewall and you are ready with nuke + t3 nukerod, bridge a chain nuke with an albatross (you can influence albatross movement by using an eye of the ocean on them), or by fog nuking which is to with floating eyes create a path where your nuke will travel to the builder instead of nearby workers which are in the fog. As an ethical titan player myself my rule to cheesing is to only do it versus players who should know better than to get caught by cheese strats. Mirror jumping someone who barely has played the map is a great way to get them to never play again.
docs.title: Mid game

docs.text: .flashlight-container { position: relative; display: inline-block; } .flashlight-image { display: block; width: 100%; max-width: 1080px; } .flashlight-overlay { position: absolute; inset: 0; pointer-events: none; } const container = document.querySelector(".flashlight-container"); const overlay = document.getElementById("overlay"); const img = document.getElementById("flashlightImg"); const BASE_WIDTH = 1080; // the max-width / design width const BASE_RADIUS = 90; // intended radius at full size function getScaledRadius() { return (img.getBoundingClientRect().width / BASE_WIDTH) * BASE_RADIUS; } function updateFlashlight(x, y) { const r = getScaledRadius(); overlay.style.background = ` radial-gradient( circle ${r}px at ${x}px ${y}px, transparent 0, transparent ${r}px, rgba(0,0,0,0.4) ${r}px, rgba(0,0,0,0.4) 100% ) `; } container.addEventListener("mousemove", (e) => { const rect = container.getBoundingClientRect(); updateFlashlight(e.clientX - rect.left, e.clientY - rect.top); }); updateFlashlight(200, 200);
docs.title: Pearl practice (lol)

docs.text: Grace period Before you even spawn in it's good to consider a few things so you can focus on instantly doing productive things. Things to consider are: Are my settings correct? bans, camera, vb, oldkeys, silence, inventory hotkeys What titan am I playing? What are my starting items with this titan? If it's random what would my starting items be? If I random a particular titan, is there an exception to what I'm buying? What is my skill build with this titan? Do I hold a skill point incase I encounter an eb? Who are the builders? Do some of the builders lumber base at the same area every game? Where am I pearling? Should I pearl before I buy my items incase I can exploit any early base? Where and when am I using my scout? If my pearl and my scout show me nothing, where would be the best direction to explore? What builder is easily abuseable for early feed? If you have the option to attack someone who played 10k games vs 1k games it's more likely the 1k game player will fail to deto fruits etc. How do I plan on getting a minion this game? This goes hand in hand with your item build. If you don't mix up your methods of getting minions you will become very predictable and might struggle after a few games once builders are aware of the trick. Early game principles Every titan early game will be different depending on what information you have and in what ways builders expose feed, but any early game should follow these principles. How you play in the early game will determine much of the rest of the game so it's important to get it right. Kill every unupgraded wall you see. Never show builders when you enter invis or break invis, unless breaking will yield a minion. Frequently put your camera at mid the first 2 minutes to see which direction builders come from, their lumber base is most likely in that direction. Put ankh on mound inventory, give it to your minion once you get one. Spend your gold as you get it, no need to jork with 300g waiting to buy a full item, buy the small components. You need at least 3x tier2 damage items to effectively kill upgraded walls, for example 2x berserker gems + titanic trident Use your lumber, cyclone is good even if builders have workers mid. If you see a lazy radioactive or nature seeding lazily in one area, it's worth to spend some time nuking there, in previous versions it wasn't but in the current version you basically have infinite mana. Tidals spawns are great, they cost 250g and if you clear one full det 6x fruit 1x RC, you've made your gold back after selling it later. Gnoll and makrura are free kills with wotw and cyclone Morphling is vulnerable to ward blocks If builders have lots of hunters you must buy runic axe and farm them Webbed feet are a nice item, but not for running around the map in fog, if you are in the fog builders can't gold and it doesn't matter if you run 20% faster if when if you do decide to show and kill walls 20% slower. Only show on the map when you want to, avoid accidentally entering vision, for example if you see a hunter which will obviously blink across some unchasable path, don't show and attack it (unless you have runic). In many cases you will be minionless at level 5, in which case you want level 3 nuke, if we don't spend the ability point at level 4 we can use it to skill level 3 nuke + unique at level 5. Once you have enough damage items you can easily control mid and clear it off from all walls, at this point you are staying permanently mid until builders stop walling, this forces builders to stay far from mound giving you a big window of opportunity where you can show on the map while builders are spending ~15 seconds to run to gold, reducing their gold income drastically. Getting a minion or exploiting builder laziness Whatever method you comitt to, for example tidal, mirror, mana mound. Know the first attempt will be the attempt most likely to succeed, once builders know you committed gold to a method they will prepare and take measures against it. Practice a play in singleplayer, not multiplayer. Item jump Item jumping into any area where builder don't expect you is usually a good idea. Go singplayer a practice jumping and picking up the item quickly so builders don't see where the jump happened, builders can still hear the item drop from quite far though. Lazy full dets A non-lazy full det needs to be immune to item jumping, mirror jumping, tidal spawns, ward jumps, teleporting to exp glyphs. If you identify a full deto lumber base which is exploitable, consider doing so. Usually there are just a few builder players able to micro workers mid to dispel cyclones, if you can remove their full dets you remove workers mid and you can cyclone builders freely. Fog tp Have a look at the teleport animation. See how on the titan the animation is just shown the first 1.5 seconds and then 2 seconds of no animation. We can abuse this if our mound is in fog, by teleporting while titan is in fog followed after the animation ends by attacking a wall at the edges of the map, and then WWing once we are teleported. The builders point of view will be: "Your allies are under attack" notifaction. Click space to see whats happening. See titan at the edge of the map and go gold. This is why it's so important for builders to always have vision of mound, to block this play. Titan can do a similar play in the early game while builders are still running mid. Before builders are running mid they are inside their walls, waiting for titan to show on the map, if we attack one side of the map builders will assume we remain in this area and builders on the opposite side of the map will feel safe, so we tp in fog and head the other direction where they are safe and ww and kill. However this all requires the mound to be in fog and the first minute after spawning is daytime so it only works if we can do it very early before the supports make it mid. Real fake tp Using a tp staff on mound will not display a status bar effect, making builders think it's a fake tp. To make this play work you need to tp in fog, but you must have recently showed on the edges of the map so builders feel safe. It's good to use the fake tp ability a couple times to condition the builders into a false sense of security. You will always spawn 5'o clock using a tp staff (assuming nothing is blocking) "Allied base is under attack bait" Similar to fog tp we can exploit builders who don't click space before golding. If use a unit such as fire spirit (spawned from fog) or tidal spawn we can force an "Allied base is under attack" notification, builders think it's titan and exit walls where we can wait in invis. For example check this. Ward jump While ward jumping is largely obsolote due to item jumping being superior, there are geometries which you can't item jump but can ward jump. Ward blocks The most reliable method to kill good players is with ward blocks, if you can get an autoattack plus nuke a 300hp builder is usually dead with enough damage items. Neutralizing wand Neut wand removes basically all buffs for 100g, it's incredibly powerful and builders don't expect their panic to be dispelled. It one shots any summoned unit. Tier 3 nuke rod / tidal scepter This is a desperation buy, if you don't have a minion and 850g to spare you can buy tidal scepter and it will together with your level 3 nuke one shot a 300hp builder. It is worth to buy for the minion but it will set your economy back 100 - 0.5*850 = -325g in a game you are already most likely struggling in. Mana mound / wotws / cyclones You can chain up to 3 cyclones or even more with using wotw from mound, cyclone ability, and wotw from titan, if there are low amount of workers mid or you expect whoever is controlling them is slow it could be a good idea. Even if the dispel is quick, it could yield a minion by delaying builders movement by just a few fractions of a second. Pheonix jumping This is how you style on noobs. As with ward jumps there is little benefit to jumping like this, but there are instances something could be jumpable with a phoenix egg or wards but not with items. Check this video to see how it's done, the video was recorded before item jump was implemented. It's preferable to jump with phoenix than with voodoo doll from mound because of the gold cost of course.
docs.title: The early game

docs.text: Before you even spawn in it's good to consider a few things so you can focus on instantly doing productive things. Things to consider are: Are my settings correct? bans, camera, vb, oldkeys, silence, inventory hotkeys What titan am I playing? What are my starting items with this titan? If it's random what would my starting items be? If I random a particular titan, is there an exception to what I'm buying? What is my skill build with this titan? Do I hold a skill point incase I encounter an eb? Who are the builders? Do some of the builders lumber base at the same area every game? Where am I pearling? Should I pearl before I buy my items incase I can exploit any early base? Where and when am I using my scout? If my pearl and my scout show me nothing, where would be the best direction to explore? What builder is easily abuseable for early feed? If you have the option to attack someone who played 10k games vs 1k games it's more likely the 1k game player will fail to deto fruits etc. How do I plan on getting a minion this game? This goes hand in hand with your item build. If you don't mix up your methods of getting minions you will become very predictable and might struggle after a few games once builders are aware of the trick.
docs.title: Grace period

docs.text: Every titan early game will be different depending on what information you have and in what ways builders expose feed, but any early game should follow these principles. How you play in the early game will determine much of the rest of the game so it's important to get it right. Kill every unupgraded wall you see. Never show builders when you enter invis or break invis, unless breaking will yield a minion. Frequently put your camera at mid the first 2 minutes to see which direction builders come from, their lumber base is most likely in that direction. Put ankh on mound inventory, give it to your minion once you get one. Spend your gold as you get it, no need to jork with 300g waiting to buy a full item, buy the small components. You need at least 3x tier2 damage items to effectively kill upgraded walls, for example 2x berserker gems + titanic trident Use your lumber, cyclone is good even if builders have workers mid. If you see a lazy radioactive or nature seeding lazily in one area, it's worth to spend some time nuking there, in previous versions it wasn't but in the current version you basically have infinite mana. Tidals spawns are great, they cost 250g and if you clear one full det 6x fruit 1x RC, you've made your gold back after selling it later. Gnoll and makrura are free kills with wotw and cyclone Morphling is vulnerable to ward blocks If builders have lots of hunters you must buy runic axe and farm them Webbed feet are a nice item, but not for running around the map in fog, if you are in the fog builders can't gold and it doesn't matter if you run 20% faster if when if you do decide to show and kill walls 20% slower. Only show on the map when you want to, avoid accidentally entering vision, for example if you see a hunter which will obviously blink across some unchasable path, don't show and attack it (unless you have runic). In many cases you will be minionless at level 5, in which case you want level 3 nuke, if we don't spend the ability point at level 4 we can use it to skill level 3 nuke + unique at level 5. Once you have enough damage items you can easily control mid and clear it off from all walls, at this point you are staying permanently mid until builders stop walling, this forces builders to stay far from mound giving you a big window of opportunity where you can show on the map while builders are spending ~15 seconds to run to gold, reducing their gold income drastically.
docs.title: Early game principles

docs.text: Whatever method you comitt to, for example tidal, mirror, mana mound. Know the first attempt will be the attempt most likely to succeed, once builders know you committed gold to a method they will prepare and take measures against it. Practice a play in singleplayer, not multiplayer.
docs.title: Getting a minion or exploiting builder laziness

docs.text: Item jumping into any area where builder don't expect you is usually a good idea. Go singplayer a practice jumping and picking up the item quickly so builders don't see where the jump happened, builders can still hear the item drop from quite far though.
docs.title: Item jump

docs.text: A non-lazy full det needs to be immune to item jumping, mirror jumping, tidal spawns, ward jumps, teleporting to exp glyphs. If you identify a full deto lumber base which is exploitable, consider doing so. Usually there are just a few builder players able to micro workers mid to dispel cyclones, if you can remove their full dets you remove workers mid and you can cyclone builders freely.
docs.title: Lazy full dets

docs.text: Have a look at the teleport animation. See how on the titan the animation is just shown the first 1.5 seconds and then 2 seconds of no animation. We can abuse this if our mound is in fog, by teleporting while titan is in fog followed after the animation ends by attacking a wall at the edges of the map, and then WWing once we are teleported. The builders point of view will be: "Your allies are under attack" notifaction. Click space to see whats happening. See titan at the edge of the map and go gold. This is why it's so important for builders to always have vision of mound, to block this play. Titan can do a similar play in the early game while builders are still running mid. Before builders are running mid they are inside their walls, waiting for titan to show on the map, if we attack one side of the map builders will assume we remain in this area and builders on the opposite side of the map will feel safe, so we tp in fog and head the other direction where they are safe and ww and kill. However this all requires the mound to be in fog and the first minute after spawning is daytime so it only works if we can do it very early before the supports make it mid.
docs.title: Fog tp

docs.text: Using a tp staff on mound will not display a status bar effect, making builders think it's a fake tp. To make this play work you need to tp in fog, but you must have recently showed on the edges of the map so builders feel safe. It's good to use the fake tp ability a couple times to condition the builders into a false sense of security. You will always spawn 5'o clock using a tp staff (assuming nothing is blocking)
docs.title: Real fake tp

docs.text: Similar to fog tp we can exploit builders who don't click space before golding. If use a unit such as fire spirit (spawned from fog) or tidal spawn we can force an "Allied base is under attack" notification, builders think it's titan and exit walls where we can wait in invis. For example check this.
docs.title: "Allied base is under attack bait"

docs.text: While ward jumping is largely obsolote due to item jumping being superior, there are geometries which you can't item jump but can ward jump.
docs.title: Ward jump

docs.text: The most reliable method to kill good players is with ward blocks, if you can get an autoattack plus nuke a 300hp builder is usually dead with enough damage items.
docs.title: Ward blocks

docs.text: Neut wand removes basically all buffs for 100g, it's incredibly powerful and builders don't expect their panic to be dispelled. It one shots any summoned unit.
docs.title: Neutralizing wand

docs.text: This is a desperation buy, if you don't have a minion and 850g to spare you can buy tidal scepter and it will together with your level 3 nuke one shot a 300hp builder. It is worth to buy for the minion but it will set your economy back 100 - 0.5*850 = -325g in a game you are already most likely struggling in.
docs.title: Tier 3 nuke rod / tidal scepter

Even if the dispel is quick, it could yield a minion by delaying builders movement by just a few fractions of a second.

docs.text: Control groups You can keybind any selection of your own or shared allied units by clicking Ctrl + 0 - 9 . This will bind that selection to that number. You can also use Shift , this will instead add to the group, compared to Ctrl which will completely override whichever selection that number currently had. You should use both methods depending on what you need, for example keybinding your towers later it's nice to use Shift to add to your selection as you build towers. I'm not an expert on micro and what the optimal control group organization is, play with whichever groups you prefer, but here are mine. [Builder] Early game 1 Builder 2 Shelter in lumber base 3 Research center 4 Initially control grouped shelter at mid which produces workers to dispel cyclones, I then shift add the units it produces to add to the control group. The result is whenever the workers die my initial shelter is still bound to this key. 5 Shelter which produces workers to gather lumber when I don't need workers dispelling at mound Late game 1 Builder 2 Ulti towers 3 Worker block shelters 4 - 9 Towers [Titan] 1 Titan 2 Mound + research center 3 Summons such as phoenix, spirit of fire, tidal Later I will put all my minions on this 4 5 6 Minions in pairs of 2
docs.title: Control Groups

docs.text: Basic Hotkeys Enter brings up the chat box and also sends the message. Esc will cancel in-progress upgrades, training, or buildings Spacebar brings you to your most recent work completed or alerts such as “We’re Under Attack!” or any other map signals too l click x2 on hero icons or Hold Click on unit-profile image to center view on the unit l click x2on a unit to select all of that specific unit type Ctrl while clicking a unit will also select all of that unit type, doing it again on a unit of the same type outside of your selection will do a new selection but units already selected will have a lower priority to join the new selection. Shift to add or remove individual units from the selected group Shift + Command will rally unit actions into a string of events Example: send builder back to harvesting lumber after finishing building or use with rally points to prevent units from getting trapped in the wrong spot Tab allows you to cycle through unit types that are selected within a group F1 F2 and F3 will select your heros Arrow keys can scroll your view/screen (same as using the mouse) Middle mouse click will let you grip and move the camera, best way to move camera as your mouse remains centered in the action Ins / Del will rotate camera angle, and the Mouse Wheel zooms in and out + and – will change the game speed in single player (online is always fastest) Alt + Q brings up the quit menu and Alt + Q Q will exit the game F10 is main menu, F11 Allies menu, F12 is the Chat Log, and F9 for Quests, the quest menu shows some interesting things such as builder/titan speech. Alt + l click will send a map ping, or signal, to your ally’s on their map radar Rally Points and Way points allow you to Queue multiple commands or actions to your units. Hold Shift to give units multiple orders in a row. Flags show up representing each of the actions that you told them to make. Unit Formation ( Alt + F ) will affect the way your units move. Units in formation move slower, at the speed of the slowest unit in the select and will align themselves. Units that are out of formation will move faster but, they will scatter around. You don't want this enabled. Inventory hotkeys are used for items. They are designated to the Numpad 1 2 4 5 7 8 . As of reforged these can now be changed, however you can't bind it to an alt / ctrl / shift modifier which makes it basically useless as these are default inventory hotkeys for other MOBAs such as DotA 2. To solve this we can remap the keys via autohotkey to whichever we want. Some people think that is cheating, but it’s not, it's just rebinding one key to another. Setting up inventory hotkeys Lazy method (1 min): Download BurnShadys precompiled autohotkey script which is found here on the ENT forums. Customizable method (10 min): Download and install autohotkey at https://www.autohotkey.com/ . Create a new file and name it whatever you want, for example my_inventoryhotkeys.ahk Make sure the file extension is ahk Open the file with any text editor, for example notepad Paste in the following autohotkey code #SingleInstance force !w::Send {Numpad8} ;Alt + w = Numpad8 !a::Send {Numpad4} ;Alt + a = Numpad4 !s::Send {Numpad5} ;Alt + s = Numpad5 !z::Send {Numpad1} ;Alt + z = Numpad1 !x::Send {Numpad2} ;Alt + x = Numpad2 !q::Send {Numpad7} ;Alt + q = Numpad7 basic autohotkey syntax ! is the symbol for alt ^ is the symbol for Ctrl + is the symbol for Shift Anything to the left of :: is the input, and anything to the right is the output. For example, !w::Send {Numpad8} means that pressing Alt + W will instead send Numpad8 . You can ask chatgpt, claude, or any other clanker if you want different keybinds. #SingleInstance force ensures that only one instance of the script can run at a time. Save and close the file Right click the file and if you installed autohotkey correctly you can choose to either run the script or to compile the script. To stop running the script right click it down in the systems tray and click exit. Default inventory keybinds vs with ahk script
docs.title: Hotkeys

docs.text: Recommended Warcraft III Settings With reforged many new settings are unlocked, most are visual and have no effect on gameplay, do whatever you wish with these settings. I'll list some settings I recommend below: Video: Window Mode: Windowed Fullscreen Sound: 3D Positional Audio: enabled Gameplay: Show Unit Life Bars: Damaged or always, i play with damaged Team Colored Life Bars: Disabled Use Special Hero Status Bars: Enabled Show Hero Level: Enabled Enhanced Tooltips: Enabled Subgroup Order Modifier Key: Enabled Enable Formation Movement Toggle: Disable Show Number For Cooldowns: Enable Show Deny Icon: Enable Hud Side Panels: Disable Background: TFT Classic (for max nostalgia) Enabled Improved Zoom: Preferential, island defense camera system is sufficient for a nice camera distance, I play with this disabled but you can enable it. Fade Occluding Objects: Enabled, trees become transparent if they are blocking the view of units. Automatically Save Replays: Enable Input: Keybinding: Classic, island defense already has gridlike hotkeys which are ergonomical. In Game Commands /fps Displays your FPS /ping Displays your ping /apm displays your APM /time displays the ingame elapsed time Host Commands !ban [name|slot] !unban [name|slot] !closeall !openall !hold [name] !holds [name] [slot] !swap [x] [y] !kick [name|slot] Player Commands !mute [name|slot] !unmute [name|slot] Island Defense Commands -cam x (-cam 135) Most players make the mistake of playing too zoomed out with cams above 170, to each their own what they prefer and it depends on the size of your monitor. I play with 135 because that's what the best player used when I started playing, I think it's necessary to accurately and quickly click in the right spots to execute jumps/blocks. -cam lock -cam scroll -ban builder1 builder2 builder3 builder4 -ban titan1 titan2 titan3 -silence (mutes builder/titan produced voice lines) -vb [on|off] (toggles vote board) -ts [on|off] (toggles titan shop UI) -vk [playername|color] (votes to kick player) -boot [playername|color] (votes to kick observer) -sh [on|off] (toggles smart heal for titan) -kick [playername|color] (for titan to boot a minion to observers) -oldkeys (swaps the dispel hotkey in gold mound from the default Q to the old bind S )
docs.title: Settings

docs.text: Unit Commands Action/Command Hotkeys – There are 5 basic actions. All of them are useful, except patrol. Actions and spells can be shift queued to perform in an order. Understanding how unit commands and attack priorities will reduce frustration when units behave in ways you don't intend. A Attack Attacks targeted unit or if targeted on ground any enemy unit on its way to the targeted spot prioritizing targets as per attack priorities listed below. P Patrol Patrols between the start position and target position, will attack targets on the way. M Move Moves to the targeted spot, if targeted on a unit it will follow the unit until it's no longer possible i.e. unit enters fog of war or dies. The move command has maphack, meaning if you order a move command across the map it will try to find the shortest path which is unblocked, if for example builders have blocked a path it will try to go around it, even if you had no knowledge this path was blocked. S Stop Stops the current and any shift queued actions. H Hold Position. Forces the unit to stand still but will attack any unit in melee range as per the priority listed below. Attack Priorities A unit whose searching for a target to attack will prioritize in the following way: Enemy hero that is attack Enemy hero nearby but not attacking Enemy units with attack command attacking Enemy units with attack command not attacking Enemy towers attacking Enemy units without attack command Enemy buildings not attacking Consequences for titan due to these priorities: Attacking a lumber base with fruits and workers, attack clicking with the titan on ground will prioritize attacking fruits. This isn't preferred because workers can be detonated so we need to manually order the titan to attack workers individually before they are detonated. Sieging a base with titan it wants to prioritize attacking the towers which are out of range so we need to individually order attack commands for each wall. A minion or titan standing outside a base will try to attack the towers attacking it, this pathing will fail and it will go close to towers until it dies or you order it to go away. Instead we use hold position for our minions / titans outside bases. A builder coming down from a wotw cyclone and there are summons attacking you in range, the titan will prioritize attacking the summons instead of the builder so we need to manually issue an attack order on the builder. Sieging a base whose worker blocking you, blocking your entrance to the base. You can hold position and the titan will priotize attacking workers in melee range. Consequences for builders: A titan sieging with minions goes invulnerable/invisible making the towers target the closest minion, basically spreading out your base damage over many units. We need to control group all our towers and issue attack commands to focus fire one unit.
docs.title: Unit commands and priority

docs.text: Worker Stacking Workers returning lumber are temporarily collisionless. As a consequence, if you spawn a worker in the window they are collisionless it will spawn ontop of the collisionless worker. Repeating this process you can stack all your workers in a tight, and preferably unnukeable area. However this process is time consuming and highly impractical, and even if you take the time to set it up fully workers sometimes get confused and refuse to return their lumber. It's good to know about it, but I don't think anyone trying to play optimally should be doing this. Nature Worker Stacking Nature workers are temporarily collisionless when they spawn in. As a consequence if you don't set a rally point on your shelter they will all stack ontop of each other. This can be used to stack spears or mystics inside your towering area of the base. Although if titan has one or more minions they will be easily nuked out before sieging and useless.
docs.title: Worker Stacking

docs.text: Armor and Attack Types Island defense has custom damage/attack types and as to not confuse us with the default wc3 types they are renamed, reskinned, and have different values. docs.text: Attributes As with the attack and defense types, island defense employs a custom attribute and leveling up system, to avoid confusion it's been renamed and reskinned to Vitality (str), Celerity (agi), and Wisdom (int). Each point of Vitality gives 50 health and 0.1 health regen per second. Celerity gives 0.25 movementspeed and 1.2% attack speed bonus. Wisdom gives 20 mana and 0.1 mana regen per second. Each level up the titanous units gain a static +10 damage and 1.5 armor per level. Note that your primary attribute doesn't give you any damage as is the case for default WC3, the tooltip says it gives you damage but it doesn't. docs.text: Item jump Item jump is another type of jump, contrary to all other jump it's hard coded into Island Defense and therefore it will not work for any other map. The system has had long history in Island Defense as it originated as a bug in the Warcraft III game engine where items dropped on the ground would not be pathable by invisible units. Melee players disliked this bug because it allowed blademaster to jump deep into enemy bases and harass their peasants so on August 8th 2018 blizzard released patch 1.30.0 which fixed this bug, and you could no longer item jump... Until April 2025 when it was added it back into the map. The same rules for cliff height applies to item jump, you can only jump to the same cliff height. Only a titan unit can jump with it, pirate and troll can try, but it won't work for them. For whoever is interested the source code is available to view in an obscure channel in the discord where it goes into detail about ranges of the jump and how it searches for target positions. However it's sufficient for us to know that when an item is dropped by an invisible titan it pushes the titan away in the opposite direction of the item drop location, as per the image below. Any type of item will jump you. Jumpable geometries Instead of grabbing our rulers and calculating distances it's more convenient to know that some geometries are jumpable and others aren't. There are 3 unique geometries which are jumpable, their mirrored counter parts are also jumpable, and they are all jumpable in any direction. For anyone wanting to prevent item jumps through their walls they need to avoid building like this. Example jump Video showing jumping each of the 3 different geometries.

docs.text: Jumping Unit collisions are a common occurrence in all Warcraft III maps, the engine is programmed for these collisions to be resolved as naturally as possible for normal melee WC3 and its campaign. However these design choices give rise to some interesting consequences. When two units collide, depending on the cause of the collision the game will attempt to separate the unit who caused the collision. The jump process is as follows. 1. Collision occurs Two units collide. The possible causes are listed later. 2. The game searches for a valid position Search begins southwest (roughly 7 o’clock) from the collision point. Search expands counterclockwise. Each full rotation increases the search radius up to a maximum distance determined by the jump type. Short jumps search up to roughly 143 range (possibly +32 units uncertainty, due to my laziness testing), slightly farther than the width of a wall. Long jumps search up to roughly 1088 range (possibly +32 units uncertainty, due to my laziness testing), roughly equivalent to 8.5 wall lengths. For a position to be valid it must meet the following criteria: Be pathable Be on the same cliff level where the collision occurred (see the ramp section for details) This cliff-level restriction is what enables many jumps. 3.a If a valid position was found The unit responsible for the collision is moved to the found location. 3.b If a valid position was not found The game will accept the units colliding. With the following exceptions Radioactive exiting a hunter The exit is prevented. Unit teleporting to a building The spell or item is consumed, but the teleport fails. Unit spawning from a building The spawn repeatedly retries until successful or canceled. An error sound and message are played for all players. Titan hunters spawning are exempt from this behavior, but resurrecting hunters are not. Below are all sources of collision, this list isn't exhaustive for all types of collisions in WC3 but it is for Island Defense as of writing this in april of 2026. If for example blade masters mirror image ability would be added it would be a long jump. Short jumps Unit exiting invisibility Unit returning lumber Radioactive exiting hunter Long jumps Unit teleporting to a building Unit spawning from building or any source such as molt ult Unit spawning in from ankh Unit exiting invisibility while being stuck in a building from mirror Unit emerging from burrow Unit entering burrow Unit emerging from tunnel Unit harvesting lumber wisp style and stops Unit building a building Example 1 This jump is interesting because it appears blocked by the shelter and builders feel safe but we can jump it. Let's break down what's happening. Preparation The mound is given voodoo ward + mana item + using regen pot to get mana. This is so it can use the voodoo ward. Titan moves into position. Ward is dropped. Titan breaks invisibility and collides with the ward The game searches for a valid spot within 143 range to place the titan, none is found and the game accepts the collision. Notice how titan is on the highround, hence why it's not being placed down towards the water. Titan attacks and kills shelter The game searches again for a valid spot and now finds where the shelter once was. The game jumps the titan. Example 2 A structure jump. Breakdown: We build 2 fruits and 3 shelters (red poorly drawn squares). The game is searching for a valid position to place our builder, as per the animation below. Starting at the violet spot. No square is valid since it's either blocked by trees or doodads or it has a different cliff height, remember we must find an area on the orange cliff height. We reach square 155 and a valid spot is found and the builder is jumped there. The other violet spot (kind of) (function() { const DELAY_MS = 130; const img = document.getElementById('base-image'); const canvas = document.getElementById('overlay'); const ctx = canvas.getContext('2d'); const BASE_WIDTH = 1080; // design width const BASE_SQ = 32; // intended square size at full width const ORIGIN = { x: 0.4, y: 0.57 }; function getScale() { return img.getBoundingClientRect().width / BASE_WIDTH; } function buildSequence() { const steps = []; let x = 0, y = 0; const dirs = [ { dx: 1, dy: 0 }, { dx: 0, dy: -1 }, { dx: -1, dy: 0 }, { dx: 0, dy: 1 }, ]; let dirIdx = 0, count = 2, phase = 0; steps.push({ gx: x, gy: y }); while (count <= 12) { const { dx, dy } = dirs[dirIdx % 4]; for (let i = 0; i < count; i++) { x += dx; y += dy; steps.push({ gx: x, gy: y }); } dirIdx++; phase++; if (phase % 2 === 0) count++; } return steps; } const sequence = buildSequence(); let drawn = 0; function syncCanvas() { canvas.width = img.offsetWidth; canvas.height = img.offsetHeight; } function redraw() { ctx.clearRect(0, 0, canvas.width, canvas.height); const scale = getScale(); const SQ = BASE_SQ * scale; const ox = ORIGIN.x * canvas.width; const oy = ORIGIN.y * canvas.height; for (let i = 0; i < drawn; i++) { const { gx, gy } = sequence[i]; const px = ox + gx * SQ, py = oy + gy * SQ; const alpha = i === drawn - 1 ? 0.85 : 0.35; ctx.save(); ctx.globalAlpha = alpha; ctx.fillStyle = '#ff9633'; ctx.fillRect(px - SQ/2, py - SQ/2, SQ, SQ); ctx.globalAlpha = Math.min(1, alpha + 0.3); ctx.strokeStyle = '#000000'; ctx.lineWidth = 2; ctx.strokeRect(px - SQ/2, py - SQ/2, SQ, SQ); ctx.globalAlpha = 1; ctx.fillStyle = '#fff'; ctx.font = `bold ${Math.round(11 * scale)}px sans-serif`; ctx.textAlign = 'center'; ctx.textBaseline = 'middle'; ctx.fillText(i + 1, px, py); ctx.restore(); } } function tick() { drawn++; redraw(); if (drawn >= sequence.length) { setTimeout(() => { drawn = 0; redraw(); }, 3000); } } function setup() { syncCanvas(); redraw(); setInterval(tick, DELAY_MS); } if (img.complete && img.naturalWidth > 0) setup(); else img.addEventListener('load', setup); window.addEventListener('resize', () => { syncCanvas(); redraw(); }); })(); Note on ethereal mirror Mirror makes buildings in its aoe ethereal and collisionless. Here's how it works: Titan casts mirror on buildings Buildings affected enter pre-mirror phase. Buildings taking any form of damage during the pre-mirror phase, from allied or enemy unit, will exit the process and not become ethereal. After a delay, dependant on the amount of nearby towers, the buildings become ethereal and collisionless. Buildings also become hostile for builders and towers will automatically attack them. Any amount of damage will make them exit ethereal state. Buildings exit ethereal state and become normal. Builders are not notified by "Our base is under attack" or "An allied base is under attack" from mirror. To counter mirror you can either be extremely attentive and autoattacking the your walls after titan cast mirror with your towers or you can blinkproof your base. Note on the WC3 search / spawn algorithm Familiarize yourself with how the search works, check the animation above or experiment yourself in game by spawning workers from a shelter without rally point. Many things in WC3 use this algorithm to search for valid spots or for units, for example when a shop determines which unit it should sell to, or how it should select units when you double click a unit, etc. The only exception for this search I've noticed is of course pathfinding but also when a worker searches for a tree to lumber from, which I found interesting why they made an exception for that. Obscure sidenote Anyone who has studied jumping might think I have cheated people out of the true explanation, which might be true, but this model is good for nearly all purposes. I intentionally left out that there are 2 "collision separators", a long one and a short one. Some actions invoke the long one, and others invoke the short one. Exiting invisibility for example invokes the short one, issuing an attack command, move command, stop command, or ability invokes the short collision separator. It was more necessary to know about this pre-2018 when the old item jump existed because you could both short and jump long with items depending on which collision separator was invoked. In the current version you might notice if you mirror walls which become collisionless, you don't need invisibility to path through them, but as they regain their collision and you are still in the walls you become stuck. This is because no collision separator was called, you could either invoke the short collision separator by for example issuing a stop command, but if there is no available space you need to invoke the long separator by exiting invisibility. Another example if you are stuck in a building by covering an entire cliff height with buildings you don't immediately jump out if some space next to where you are stuck is emptied, but if you exit invis you can invoke the long separator or if you issue a stop or attack command you can invoke the short one.

docs.text: Cliff heights A Warcraft III map is divided into discrete cliff heights. When a unit is jumping, it cannot move to a different cliff height. This restriction is fundamental to how jumps behave. Anatomy of a ramp A ramp connects two different cliff heights. While ramps may appear visually different, they all fundamentally work the same. A ramp is 256 units long (the length of two walls): The first 192 units are considered low ground The remaining 64 units are considered high ground A unit or building whose center is inside the transition zone is treated as being on the low ground. However, units in this area are granted vision up the remainder of the ramp and an additional 128 units onto the high ground. Ramp vision block Using our understanding of ramps and building centers, we can create situations where a building blocks vision across a ramp. Consider a 4x4 building (256x256) placed so that it covers both the transition zone and the high ground. Depending on the ramps orientation: - In two orientations, the building center remains outside the ramp - In the other two, the center lies inside the ramp Based on the terrain rules described earlier, all four cases would be expected to block vision equally. However, only the orientations where the building center lies outside the ramp fully block vision. I don't have a satisfactory explanation for this, one can only assume blizzard hardcoded that you are able to see any building whose center is on the ramp.
docs.title: Cliff Height and Ramps

docs.text: Shelter mechanics In certain tree geometries when you have 2 shelters too close to each other, harvesters can get confused to which shelter they return lumber to and become stuck. The rule to avoid this is to build your shelters from bottom to top, or from left to right. Check video for reference, first example is correct and second is incorrect and a harvester becomes stuck. This is obscure knowledge from gogblitz of the vampirism community. It is important to know about this mechanic if you are ever basing in hermit or stronghold for example, it's a good habit to always try to follow this rule as in my experience workers misbehave a lot when they become collisionless from returning lumber. If you don't follow this rule your lumber income will be reduced as the bugged worker will remain idle until you force it to return lumber and for faerie it's even more crucial as her workers don't have an animation you will never notice that they are idle. Here's the build order for stronghold and hermit.
docs.title: Shelter Mechanics

docs.text: Grid and unit sizes A warcraft III map is divided into small squares, in game while placing buildings you can't place buildings at its true resolution, you can only build every 64 units, not every 32 units. Each square is 32x32 units, so a wall is 128x128. It's impractical to refer to sizes this way so we call a wall being of size 2x2. It's still good to be aware of the true length because it indicates the range of abilities, attacks, vision etc. A unit with movespeed of 300 means it will move 300 units in one second. Unit/building dimensions There are 6 sizes any unit or building has. See the picture below. List of different relevant units who has these sizes, range units / intuitive units 32x32 / 0.5x0.5 Mini-minions, items dropped on the ground (these are pathable, just unbuildable) 64x64 / 1x1 Builders, wards, tidal spawns, phoenix egg 96x96 / 1.5x1.5 Titan (contrary to what we tell noobs, titan isn't 2x2), minions, hunters, spirit of fire 128x128 / 2x2 Walls, towers 192x192 / 3x3 Fruits, arc 256x256 / 4x4 Merch, rc Center of buildings and units Each 32x32 square on the grid has a cliff height, a unit or building will be on the cliff height its center is on. It's not obvious for all unit sizes which square will be the center but you can figure it out experimentally. Below is the center for all sizes. This is important to understand for buildings built on the border on ramps as they will only be able to spawn units on the same cliff height which the center square is on, same for orc style builders are considered to be on the center square while constructing their building.
